faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Got questions? We've got answers. Find everything you need to know about using our platform, plans, and features.

industries.question: Do you have GDPR complience?
industries.answer: Yes. Clinical Data Studio fully supports GDPR requirements for personal data protection. Our platform automatically detects and anonymizes PII according to GDPR standards, ensuring your organization meets EU data protection obligations while maintaining full data utility for analysis and research.

customSolutions.question: Do you have HIPPA complience?
customSolutions.answer: Yes. We provide full HIPAA compliance support with two de-identification methods: Safe Harbor (removing all 18 PHI identifiers) and Expert Determination (statistical risk-based approach with justification). Both methods produce audit-ready compliance reports accepted by healthcare regulatory bodies.

support.question: Can I export the anonymized or synthetic data after processing?
support.answer: Yes. After processing, you can download your anonymized or synthetic data in PDF format. A compliance report with full audit trail is also available for download directly from the results screen.

implementation.question: What are the pricing plans?
implementation.answer: We offer a free trial so you can explore the platform before purchasing. For enterprise pricing and volume-based plans, reach out to our team and we'll find the best option for your organization.

settings.title: Configure De-Identification
settings.subtitle: Choose how patient data will be anonymized under HIPAA
settings.subtitleStandard: Select a privacy risk level or customize how each entity type is handled

detection.title: Detection sensitivity
detection.subtitle: Controls how carefully the system detects sensitive data.
detection.recommendedNote: Balanced is recommended for most documents.

conservative.title: Conservative
conservative.info: Detects only obvious identifiers. Best for structured data.

balanced.title: Balanced
balanced.info: Detects most sensitive entities while keeping false positives manageable.

aggressive.title: Aggressive
aggressive.info: Catches potentially sensitive data — may include false positives

medium.title: Medium
medium.description: Balanced privacy and utility. Meets standard GDPR guidelines.

euGdpr.description: Methods are automatically selected based on GDPR risk guidelines and entity sensitivity levels. You can review or override any setting below.
euGdpr.warning: Low risk may not fully protect sensitive health data. Biological data and photos will still be removed (Art. 9).

ukGdpr.description: Methods are automatically selected based on UK GDPR guidelines and entity sensitivity levels. You can review or override any setting below.
ukGdpr.warning: Low risk may not fully protect sensitive health data. Biological data and photos will still be removed (UK GDPR Art. 9).

swissFadp.description: Methods are automatically selected based on Swiss FADP guidelines and entity sensitivity levels. You can review or override any setting below.
swissFadp.warning: Low risk may not fully protect sensitive health data. Biological data and photos will still be removed (FADP Art. 5).

default.description: Methods are automatically selected based on regulatory guidelines. You can review or override any setting below.
default.warning: Low risk may not fully protect sensitive health data.

entityConfig.title: Entity configuration
entityConfig.chooseIdentifiers: Choose which identifiers to remove from your document.
entityConfig.deselectedByDefault: All categories are deselected by default

resetDialog.title: Reset to Medium defaults?
resetDialog.message: Your custom settings will be lost.

logicDrawer.title: Configuration Logic
logicDrawer.subtitle: Methods are automatically selected based on GDPR risk level. You can review how each entity is processed below.

euGdpr.label: GDPR Art. 9
euGdpr.notice: These entity types are legally protected under GDPR Article 9. Only Remove is permitted.

ukGdpr.label: UK GDPR Art. 9
ukGdpr.notice: These entity types are legally protected under UK GDPR Article 9. Only Remove is permitted.

swissFadp.label: FADP Art. 5
swissFadp.notice: These entity types are legally protected under FADP Article 5. Only Remove is permitted.

default.notice: These entity types are subject to strict legal protection. Only Remove is permitted.

person.title: Person
person.description: Assigns a consistent token, allows re-linking if legally required

organization.title: Organization
organization.description: Hides part of the value if organisation is considered sensitive

dateTime.title: Date/Time
dateTime.description: Converts DOB to age range (e.g. 40–50). Timestamps rounded to month or quarter

location.title: Location
location.description: Reduces to region or 3-digit postcode. EDPB recommends geographic generalisation

ip.title: IP
ip.description: Last segment of the IP address is removed. Only the network range is kept.

geopoint.title: Geopoint
geopoint.description: Exact coordinates rounded to city or regional centroid

nationalId.title: National_ID
nationalId.description: One-way removal. No plaintext mapping stored in dataset

creditCard.title: Credit_Card
creditCard.description: Removes full numbers. BIN or last 4 digits may be kept only if business-necessary.

email.title: Email
email.description: Full address and number removed. Domain may be preserved if business-necessary

mrn.title: Medical_Record_Number
mrn.description: Stable token assigned. Mapping stored server-side under access control

deviceId.title: Device_ID
deviceId.description: One-way encryption with salt. Keys stored under strict access control

freeText.title: Free_Text (PHI)
freeText.description: NLP model scans unstructured text. Manual QA recommended before sharing

biologicalData.title: Biological_Data
biologicalData.description: Treated as highest-risk data. No alternative methods permitted

photo.title: Photo / Image
photo.description: Biometric data. Removal mandatory under Art. 9

token.title: Replace with token — Stable pseudonym
token.description: Assigns a consistent token, allows re-linking

generalise.title: Generalise — Reduce precision
generalise.description: Converts to broader category or range

pseudonymise.title: Pseudonymise — Store mapping
pseudonymise.description: Replaces with token, mapping stored securely

nlp.title: NLP redaction — Free text PHI
nlp.description: Applies NLP model to unstructured text

identifiers.title: All 18 HIPAA identifiers will be removed
identifiers.description: This includes names, dates, contact details, account numbers, medical identifiers, biometric data, and other HIPAA-defined identifiers.
identifiers.customizeSubtitle: Select which identifiers you want to remove
identifiers.fixedSubtitle: All identifiers are fixed in Safe Harbor mode
identifiers.expertTooltip: Switch to Expert Determination to customize

results.title: De-Identification Results
results.failedToGenerate: Something went wrong while processing the document. Please try again.
results.originalSubtitle: Original text with detected entities highlighted

syntheticData.title: Synthetic Data Generator
syntheticData.subtitle: Generate realistic but completely fake clinical data for development
syntheticData.completeRequiredFields: Complete required fields to generate dataset
